core_mechanism: decomposition of a system's transient relaxation into a superposition of exponentially-damped sinusoidal normal modes (complex-frequency resonances), whose oscillation frequencies and decay rates are estimated to infer the system's few underlying parameters and test the governing model
brief_reason: The abstract treats the signal as a superposition of quasi-normal modes that are damped harmonic oscillations whose frequencies and damping times are fixed by two system parameters, and it fits those frequencies/damping times to constrain the model — i.e. damped-oscillation (Prony-type) spectral estimation of complex resonant modes.
